commit.message:
Bring the blog closer to a calmer editorial reference

This pass pulls the visual system closer to the kawe.ski-style editorial
reference while keeping the homepage interaction web intact. The shell
now uses a darker base, softer panel contrast, mono micro-labels, and a
single high-contrast OKLCH accent instead of multiple competing hues.

The interaction web still uses Pretext for measured note labels and card
sizing, but the surrounding presentation is more restrained. That makes
room for typography and interaction to carry the page instead of extra
motion or ornamental gradients.

Constraint: The note-web interaction remained the primary homepage feature
Constraint: The redesign had to stay inside the existing static build pipeline
Rejected: Keep the lighter cream-first palette | it drifted too far from the requested dark/high-contrast direction
Rejected: Add more animation during the visual pass | typography and interaction were already the stronger signal
Confidence: medium
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Preserve the single-accent discipline; if more color is added later, keep it secondary to the OKLCH teal highlight
Tested: node --check static/assets/pretext-note-web.mjs; cargo fmt --all; cargo clippy -q --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test -q; cargo run -q -p blog-build -- --site-url https://example.test --title 'Jinhyuk Kim — Signals & Notes' --description 'AI research notes, connected writing, and daily news radar.'; local agent-browser screenshots for home top and interaction-web section
Not-tested: Live-site review after push
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Support JSON.jl 0.21 and 1 (#31)

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex GPT-5 <codex@openai.com>

Remove dead shell code and tighten the note-web interaction path

This cleanup pass trims leftover slop from the recent redesign work
without changing behavior. Dead ambient markup and unused global CSS
selectors are gone, the page-mode overrides are expressed once instead
of repeated across four page classes, and the note-web detail state now
uses one small helper instead of repeating the same DOM update logic.

Constraint: Cleanup stayed bounded to the recent homepage/base CSS/JS surface
Constraint: No behavior changes to the interaction web or reading pages
Rejected: Broader style-system rewrite | beyond the scope of a slop pass
Rejected: JS architecture changes in the note-web layout solver | not justified by the current smells
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep future cleanup passes bounded; prefer deleting dead selectors/markup over introducing new helper layers
Tested: node --check static/assets/pretext-note-web.mjs; cargo fmt --all; cargo clippy -q --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test -q; cargo run -q -p blog-build -- --site-url https://example.test --title 'Jinhyuk Kim — Signals & Notes' --description 'AI research notes, connected writing, and daily news radar.'
Not-tested: Live-site browser pass after push
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(import): handle phone uniqueness conflicts + speed up preview progress (#1093)

* fix(import): handle phone uniqueness conflicts during order commit + speed up preview progress

Two fixes for the import job pipeline:

1. Customer phone dedup (resolve-import-customer.ts): Fix customers_merchant_phone_unique
   constraint violation during order commit. Phone-only orders now link to existing phone
   holders with email/user_id. Email-identified orders INSERT with phone=null when phone
   is already taken. Safety net retries INSERT without phone on concurrent 23505 errors.

2. Preview progress cold-start (import-job-service.ts): Report totalRows to the UI as
   soon as CSV is parsed, before existing orders/products DB queries finish. This closes
   the gap where the UI shows 0/0 while waiting for unrelated DB loads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(import): improve retry error message and fix phone cache corruption

- Include original insertError.message as fallback when retryError is null
- Only update customersByPhone cache using the actual inserted phone value,
  not the original order phone (which may have been nulled out to avoid
  the uniqueness constraint)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(import): tighten phone match guard and handle phone-only concurrent collisions

- Change allPhoneMatches guard from length > 0 to === 1 to avoid ambiguous merges
- For phone-only orders hitting 23505, look up existing phone holder instead of
  inserting a null-phone row that would lose the phone identifier
- Email-identified orders still retry INSERT with phone=null as before

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Strip the presentation back to a simpler publication baseline

The interaction web idea is better than the previous decorative passes,
but the surrounding shell still carried too much visual baggage. This
reset removes the leftover kinetic-home scripts and rewrites the global,
note, tag, and graph styles around a quieter baseline so the content and
note-to-note interaction become the primary visual focus.

The result is a lighter, flatter publication system: simple surfaces,
clean borders, calmer navigation, and less ornamental contrast. The home
interaction web remains, while the rest of the site now shares a much
more restrained reading-first style.

Constraint: The homepage note-web behavior had to keep working while the rest of the styling was simplified
Constraint: Existing routes and server-side templates remained the source of truth
Rejected: Keep the dark glossy shell and only tweak colors | still felt over-designed
Rejected: Remove the note web as well | it was the one interaction direction the user explicitly wanted to keep
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Add visual complexity only when it directly improves note discovery or reading; avoid standalone flourishes
Tested: node --check static/assets/pretext-note-web.mjs; cargo fmt --all; cargo clippy -q --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test -q; cargo run -q -p blog-build -- --site-url https://example.test --title 'Jinhyuk Kim — Signals & Notes' --description 'AI research notes, connected writing, and daily news radar.'; local agent-browser screenshots for home top, interaction web, and note page; local eval verifying note-web hover updates the detail panel
Not-tested: Production view after this minimal reset push

Center the homepage around a simpler note interaction web

The previous kinetic pass was still too styled and too self-conscious for
what the blog needed. This revision strips the homepage back to a
clearer introduction, a focused note-web panel, and straightforward
secondary lists so the interaction itself does the work instead of a lot
of decorative chrome.

Each markdown note on the landing page is now represented by a
Pretext-sized card inside a local interaction web. Labels are measured
with Pretext, links are drawn from the real graph data, and hovering a
note highlights its neighbors while updating the side detail panel.

Constraint: Existing note routes and graph data had to remain the source of truth
Constraint: The homepage needed to degrade cleanly without changing the markdown pipeline
Rejected: Keep the animated hero as the homepage focal point | it distracted from the notes themselves
Rejected: Fall back to a simple card list on home | it would not deliver the interaction-web behavior the user asked for
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep the homepage interaction web legible first; if more motion is added later, do not let it obscure note titles or link structure
Tested: node --check static/assets/pretext-note-web.mjs; cargo fmt --all; cargo clippy -q --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test -q; cargo run -q -p blog-build -- --site-url https://example.test --title 'Jinhyuk Kim — Signals & Notes' --description 'AI research notes, connected writing, and daily news radar.'; local agent-browser screenshots of the homepage interaction web
Not-tested: Production interaction-web behavior before the next deploy completes

commit.message:
Optimize row-isolated polygon clip and difference dispatch

- route single-pair polygon intersection through row-isolated overlay to hit same-row candidate generation
- share exact single-row polygon difference correction between the constructive dispatcher and kernel wrapper
- keep grouped polygon difference batches on the rowwise GPU path and add regressions for mixed polygon families and overlay assembly parity

Profile Summary:
- join-heavy:dissolve_groups 0.07s
- constructive:write_output 0.07s
- predicate-heavy:read_geojson 0.07s load_polygons 0.01s point_in_polygon 0.01s
- zero-transfer:read_input 0.04s subset_rows 0.01s write_output 0.08s

commit.message:
Turn the blog into a kinetic editorial surface

The previous pass improved structure but still looked like a themed app.
This rework pushes the blog into a much more opinionated publication
shape: dark shell, paper reading surfaces, a stronger hierarchy, and a
homepage cover that behaves like an animated editorial spread instead of
just another content dashboard.

Pretext now does visibly heavier lifting on the landing page. The hero
headline is rendered as measured lines inside a custom stage with moving
orb accents, while the masonry stream keeps using measured copy for
stable card packing. I also added a small reveal layer so archive and
news cards settle into place instead of appearing flatly.

Constraint: Content, routes, and markdown rendering had to stay intact
Constraint: The redesign had to reuse the existing static Rust pipeline
Rejected: Keep iterating on the earlier pastel card design | it still read like a template rather than a publication
Rejected: Animate the entire markdown article body with Pretext | too risky for readability and accessibility
Confidence: medium
Scope-risk: broad
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep Pretext concentrated on headline, card, and accent surfaces; if you expand the animated layout further, preserve no-JS fallbacks and readability first
Tested: cargo fmt --all; cargo clippy -q --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test -q; cargo run -q -p blog-build -- --site-url https://example.test --title 'Jinhyuk Kim — Signals & Notes' --description 'AI research notes, connected writing, and daily news radar.'; node --check static/assets/pretext-home-hero.mjs; node --check static/assets/pretext-masonry.mjs; node --check static/assets/editorial-motion.js; local agent-browser screenshots for home, note, and tag pages
Not-tested: Production behavior on mud-blog.pages.dev after this second-pass rework push
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

🐛 fix(workspace): clean legacy manual session workspaces

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(skills): switch review fallback to codex subagent

Summary:
- replace the temporary local Claude CLI review gate with a Codex
  subagent workflow in the review skill
- keep the existing gate name and review loop semantics so callers do
  not need to change trigger points
- tighten the pass criteria so `NO_P0` is emitted only when there are
  no blocking findings and subagent startup failures remain blocking

Rationale:
- local Claude review is currently unavailable in this environment, so
  the fallback path should be explicit and operational
- review gate instructions must not silently bypass the second-pass
  review or accidentally approve blocking findings

Tests:
- subagent re-review of .agents/skills/claude-review-codex/SKILL.md
  returned NO_FINDINGS

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Keep the landing surface focused on reading instead of chrome

A live pass on mud-blog.pages.dev showed the new shell working, but the
home page was still fighting itself: the hero repeated the site title too
aggressively and the right-side graph rail compressed the main layout.
I trimmed the hero copy to a steadier message and removed the side graph
rail from home and tag pages so the editorial stream can breathe.

The test fixture was updated to match the new home-page contract and to
assert the masonry script is present where the redesigned listing now
relies on it.

Constraint: The note pages kept their graph/toc context rail because it remains useful while reading

feat(release): harden setup and OTLP diagnostics for 0.1.56

Summary:
- add active `spanory probe otlp`, structured OTLP status persistence,
  and doctor checks for probe plus last-session metrics
- ship one-click setup, for-agent release assets, packaged plugin
  payloads, and metrics endpoint override support in release flows
- simplify enduser id resolution to explicit env override and align
  packaged runtime setup for Claude, OpenClaw, OpenCode, and Codex

Rationale:
- fail fast on OTLP trace and metrics misconfiguration during setup
  instead of discovering upload gaps after the fact
- make token metric generation and upload state diagnosable from local
  status files and doctor output
- keep packaged installs stable on macOS by retrying newly installed
  binaries before probe and doctor execution

Tests:
- npm run check
- npm test
- npm run --workspace @bububuger/spanory test -- otlp.probe.spec.ts
  setup.spec.ts otlp.spec.ts
- bash scripts/release/package-release-assets.sh v0.1.56 dist
  dist/release/0.1.56
- E2E: local setup/install/probe/export/doctor against a mock OTLP
  server and local release archive

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Make the notebook read like a finished publication

The redesign keeps the markdown posts and news digests intact while
rebuilding the presentation layer around a more editorial shell. The
home page now acts like a magazine front page, note pages have a more
intentional hero/body/backlinks rhythm, and tag/graph/error views now
share the same visual language.

Pretext is now used in two meaningful places: line-level copy treatment
for key labels and headings, plus a measured masonry layout for home and
tag card streams. I also extended the test fixture coverage so the new
editorial assets are locked into the build output.

Constraint: Post and news content/routes had to remain unchanged
Constraint: No new runtime dependency was added for the redesign
Rejected: Rebuild markdown rendering around client-side JS | would have broken the static-first pipeline
Rejected: CSS-only masonry for cards | would not use Pretext meaningfully
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep Pretext focused on presentation surfaces; do not move article body rendering into a JS-only layout path without rechecking accessibility and static fallback behavior
Tested: cargo fmt --all; cargo clippy -q --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test -q; cargo run -q -p blog-build -- --site-url https://example.test --title 'Jinhyuk Kim — Signals & Notes' --description 'AI research notes, connected writing, and daily news radar.'; node --check static/assets/pretext-masonry.mjs; Playwright screenshots for home and post pages
Not-tested: Production deployment on mud-blog.pages.dev after push

commit.message:
Merge PR #2: Julia MCWF migration

### Motivation

- Port the MATLAB Monte Carlo wavefunction (MCWF / MC quantum jumps) implementation to Julia using `QuantumOptics.jl` and provide a high-performance, thread-parallel core optimized for production runs and comparisons with the MATLAB reference.
- Provide reproducible project configuration and developer tooling (instantiation, benchmark, MATLAB-compatibility verification, and a Jupyter entry notebook) to ease validation and server-scale parameter scans.

### Description

- Add a new Julia package structure with `Project.toml` and `Manifest.toml` and a top-level module `src/QuantumMC.jl` that exposes the `MCWFOptimized` submodule.
- Implement the MCWF core in `src/MCWFOptimized.jl` including `MCWFParams`/`RunConfig` types, per-thread `TrajCache`, `run_trajectory!` single-trajectory logic, and `run_ensemble` threaded execution with thread-local RNGs and caches.
- Fix numerical/physics issues versus the original port: use the full kinetic energy expression including the cross term, handle possibly-negative discriminants by converting to `ComplexF64` in `_ep32`, and perform in-place, allocation-free operations (`mul!`, `copyto!`, preallocated buffers) to reduce GC pressure.
- Add documentation, usage and operational guides in `README.md` and `docs/` (Chinese), a Jupyter notebook `notebooks/QuantumMC_入口.ipynb`, and utility scripts `scripts/verify_against_matlab.jl` and `scripts/benchmark.jl` for correctness verification against a MATLAB `.mat` reference and for benchmarking (including optional MATLAB `parfor` speedup comparison).

### Testing

- This PR does not include CI test runs and no automated tests were executed as part of the push.
- Added verification and benchmark helpers: run `scripts/verify_against_matlab.jl` (requires a `matlab_reference.mat` with `probability_ref`) for correctness and `scripts/benchmark.jl` for performance measurement; these scripts are intended for local/CI execution.
- Package instantiation is supported via `julia --project -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.instantiate()'` to prepare dependencies before running the verification or benchmark scripts.
- No test failures were reported because no automated tests were run in this PR.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Unify harness UI workbench structure

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Mark keyboard and shell as implemented

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Make the blog feel like one coherent publication

This change turns the recent Pretext enhancement work into a cohesive
site-wide visual system instead of a set of isolated page tweaks.
It vendors the Pretext runtime for progressive text layout on safe
plain-text blocks, updates the templates to expose those targets,
and redesigns home, post, archive, graph, and sidebar surfaces around
one editorial paper-plus-glass language.

I also cleaned the new CSS before landing it by pulling repeated hero
chip rules into shared styles and trimming page-specific duplication,
so the current look is easier to extend without reintroducing visual
branching.

Constraint: Keep the static Rust/Askama architecture and existing information layout
Constraint: Must preserve rich embeds, math, diagrams, and other non-plain-text content
Rejected: Full template/layout rewrite | too broad for the current push-first delivery
Rejected: Re-render all article HTML through Pretext | unsafe for nested rich markup
Confidence: medium
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Extend future polish through the shared panel/chip system before adding page-only style dialects
Tested: cargo test

Add classification and Drive sync toolkit

Expand the repository beyond the tl;dv exporter by adding project classification, Drive archive publishing, and project-folder synchronization workflows. Document the full pipeline, provide a reusable rules template, and sanitize the public-facing defaults so the package can be shared without internal project names or folder IDs.

commit.message:
fix(push): explicit params to fix stale closure + remove Device.isDevice guard (#1095)

* fix(push): pass explicit userId/merchantId to avoid stale closures

React Compiler memoization causes registerPush() closures to capture
stale user/merchant values. Pass them explicitly from the layout effects
where the values are known to be current.

Also removes Device.isDevice guard that falsely blocks real TestFlight
devices when expo-device is dynamically imported.

Work (#9)

* Add contacts import (Google Sheets/YClients), DB migrations, and clients/staff/settings UI/hooks updates

### Motivation
- Provide a robust path to import contacts from either YClients or Google Sheets and persist import metadata in org settings.
- Make clients UI more feature-rich (age/discount filters, wider table with left-accent scrollbar) and keep staff UI in sync with real masters data from the DB.
- Ensure database schema supports YClients-compatible client fields and automatic linking between user records and clients when YClients IDs appear.

### Description
- Added an API endpoint `app/api/clients/import/route.ts` that normalizes rows from `yclients` or `google_sheets`, upserts/inserts into `clients`, and writes import metadata into `org_settings`.
- Implemented `lib/imports/normalizeGoogleSheetsClient.ts` to adapt Google Sheets rows into the clients schema and added `normalizeYclientsRow` logic in the import route.
- Introduced DB migrations and helper SQL: `add_contacts_import_source.sql`, `add_yclients_clients_columns.sql`, `link_users_to_clients_on_yc_id.sql`, seed and reset updates and a seeds file `seed_test_masters.sql` to populate masters for staff cards.
- Updated frontend: extended clients filters and table (`app/clients/page.tsx`) with age/discount filters, redesigned table layout and local left-accent scrollbar (`app/globals.css`), added settings UI to pick and save import source (`app/settings/page.tsx`), and disabled/marked in-development auto-systems in `app/system/page.tsx`.
- Refactored data hooks: `lib/hooks/useClients.ts` now reads from `clients` and normalizes new fields (discount, channels, birthday, etc.), and added a new `lib/hooks/useYclientsMasters.ts` hook to fetch/subscribe to `masters` for the staff UI; `app/staff/page.tsx` updated to consume masters and render realistic KPIs and cards.

### Testing
- Ran TypeScript/Next.js build (`next build`) and static type-checking locally and the build completed without type errors.
- Ran linter/format checks (ESLint) and no lint errors were reported for modified files.
- Performed basic automated smoke checks by running the dev server and loading `Clients`, `Staff`, and `Settings` pages to ensure hooks and new components render without runtime errors (no automated unit tests were changed or present for these new features).

* Store messenger user IDs on clients during yc_id auto-link

* Add reverse sync from clients to messenger user tables

---------

* Bump version to 1.1.0 with multilingual help support

* Release v1.1.1: multilingual polish, 144 fresh screenshots, token-stability fix

- Fix fr.json UTF-8 mojibake (176 corrupted accented characters restored)
- Add missing menu.rename / menu.delete / menu.deleteAllConversations keys to de, fr, it, ko, uk
- Fix LocalApiService.Start() to call EnsureApiAccessToken() so the local API token
  is always present in appstate_strings.json on startup, even after a file wipe
- Fix LanguageHelper.SetAppStateLanguage() to merge into the existing JSON instead of
  overwriting it, preserving the apiAccessToken and other keys
- Retake all 144 help screenshots (12 languages × 12 screens) under Assets/Help/{lang}/
- Remove stale root-level and extra per-language screenshot files
- Update help-assets-plan.json: add languageBatch with all 12 languages, preBatchActions

  window-title selectors (Settings — Aire, Welcome to Aire)

docs: reprioritize next round optimization plan

Summary:
- record the flow-core work already landed in this round, including pipeline/runtime/graph and TaskGroup changes
- replace the old phase ordering with the new evidence-driven priority order
- mark low-yield and high-risk branches as paused unless new measurements justify them

Rationale:
- the latest benchmark runs moved fibonacci down materially, but pipeline and DAG scheduling remain the dominant gaps
- keeping the written plan aligned with the current code and measurement state avoids wasting the next implementation batch

Validation:
- plan update only

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

perf(flow-core): tighten pipeline and task-group hot paths

Summary:
- make RuntimeJoinScope and TaskGroup state lazy, and route TaskGroup children through a lighter dedicated path
- cache graph pending state and add a small inline-graph fast path to cut executor setup overhead
- reduce pipeline serial-stage hot-path overhead and add regression coverage for token accounting and ordering

Rationale:
- pipeline and recursive task orchestration were still the biggest validated hot spots in the latest comparison runs
- isolating the verified flow-core changes now keeps the next pipeline-focused round easier to measure and revert if needed

Validation:
- cargo test -p flow-core
- cargo run --release -p benchmarks --bin taskflow_compare -- --threads 4 --rounds 3 --cases fibonacci --output-dir benchmarks/reports/taskflow_compare/task_group_batch_only_fibonacci_20260406
- cargo run --release -p benchmarks --bin taskflow_compare -- --threads 4 --rounds 3 --cases data_pipeline --output-dir benchmarks/reports/taskflow_compare/pipeline_after_runtime_batch_revert_data_only_20260406

Work (#8)

* Integrate YClients data, enhance clients table & filters, add masters hook and DB migrations

### Motivation
- Migrate client handling toward YClients-compatible schema and enrich client filtering and table presentation for better audience selection.
- Replace mocked masters data with real `masters` rows and surface master KPIs in the Staff page.
- Improve UX for wide clients table with a persistent header, local accent scrollbar and scroll reset behavior.

### Description
- Add new numeric filters and columns to clients: `minAge`, `maxAge`, `minDiscount`, `maxDiscount`, display `discount` and `birthday` in the clients table, and restructure the table into a horizontally scrollable container with a sticky header in `app/clients/page.tsx`.
- Update client normalization and source table in `lib/hooks/useClients.ts` to map more YClients-like fields, add `discount` to the `Client` type, adapt channel mapping, and change Supabase queries/subscription from `clients_tg` to `clients`.
- Introduce `useYclientsMasters` hook (`lib/hooks/useYclientsMasters.ts`) to fetch/normalize `masters` from Supabase and update `app/staff/page.tsx` to consume this hook (replace mock data, compute totals, cards and KPI rows from live data).
- Add UI polish and behavior: left-accent local scrollbar styles in `app/globals.css`, `clientsTableScrollRef` scroll-reset effect, and segment -> status mapping for table badges.
- Add DB migration/seed SQL: `supabase/add_yclients_clients_columns.sql` to add needed YClients-compatible columns to `public.clients`, and `supabase/seed_test_masters.sql` to seed sample masters safely.
- Mark certain automation systems as "in development" and disable controls for them in `app/system/page.tsx` via `IN_DEVELOPMENT_SYSTEM_CODES`.

### Testing
- Ran TypeScript type-check (`tsc --noEmit`) and a local project build verification (`next build`) to validate typings and bundling, both completed without errors.
- No new unit tests were added in this change set and existing test suites were not modified.

* Add DB sync from messenger users to clients by yc_id

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(csr): add separate staff login flow (#24)

Summary:
- replace the landing page greeting form with a CSR-aware portal entry
- add dedicated CSR login and dashboard routes with isolated session storage
- cover portal entry, login, redirect, and logout behavior with node tests

Rationale:
- CSR authentication needs its own routes and session boundary before
  customer authentication is introduced
- the protected dashboard path must deny unauthenticated access and
  return authenticated staff directly to their queue entry point

Tests:
- npm test
